title: AuthorityRegistry
description: Structured registry of educational authorities in the Indian and Maharashtra education ecosystems.

MoE.name: Ministry of Education (India)
MoE.role: Central governing body for education policy and budget allocations in India.
MoE.authority_scope: National policy framing, final approval of central funding, setting national educational indicators.
MoE.authority_limits: Cannot directly mandate implementation in state-run schools due to education being a Concurrent List subject under the Indian Constitution.
MoE.announcement_authority: High (direct ministerial decrees, national policy releases like NEP).
MoE.policy_authority: High (author of national curriculum frameworks, guidelines).
MoE.operational_authority: Medium-Low (limited to central institutions like Kendriya Vidyalayas and Navodaya Vidyalayas).
MoE.procurement_authority: Medium-High (procures national-scale educational infrastructure like DIKSHA hosting).
MoE.deployment_authority: Low (must coordinate with State Departments for state-wide rollouts).
MoE.known_ambiguity_regions: Overlaps with State Departments regarding state-level curriculum adaptations under the Concurrent List.
MoE.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on state governments for on-the-ground implementation and local school alignment.

NCERT.name: National Council of Educational Research and Training
NCERT.role: Academic advisory body for curriculum and textbook development.
NCERT.authority_scope: Designs model textbooks, curates the National Curriculum Framework (NCF), conducts educational research.
NCERT.authority_limits: Advisory body only. Cannot force state boards or schools to adopt its textbooks or syllabus.
NCERT.announcement_authority: Medium (announces model syllabus versions, NCF drafts).
NCERT.policy_authority: High (intellectual authorship of NCF, textbook standards).
NCERT.operational_authority: Low (no administrative control over any schools).
NCERT.known_ambiguity_regions: No authority to force private schools to use NCERT books, despite CBSE preferences.
NCERT.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on the MoE for final NCF approvals and state boards for local SCERT adoptions.

CBSE.name: Central Board of Secondary Education
CBSE.role: National school board affiliating public and private schools.
CBSE.authority_scope: Conducts board examinations for classes 10 and 12, sets affiliation norms, mandates NCERT syllabus for affiliated schools.
CBSE.authority_limits: Bound strictly to affiliated schools. Cannot regulate state board schools.
CBSE.announcement_authority: High (board exam schedules, curriculum updates for affiliated schools).
CBSE.policy_authority: Medium (implements NCERT policy guidelines, sets school bylaws).
CBSE.operational_authority: High (affiliates schools, conducts exams, issues certificates).
CBSE.known_ambiguity_regions: Jurisdiction over private CBSE schools located in states with strong regional board mandates.
CBSE.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on NCERT for textbook contents and MoE for national policy mandates.

AICTE.name: All India Council for Technical Education
AICTE.role: Statutory body for planning and coordinated development of technical education.
AICTE.authority_scope: Approves engineering, management, and pharmacy colleges; mandates engineering curricula and credit frameworks.
AICTE.authority_limits: Strictly limited to technical education. No authority over general arts, science, or commerce colleges.
AICTE.policy_authority: High (technical education credit frameworks, AI/ML curriculum mandates).
AICTE.known_ambiguity_regions: Friction with UGC regarding jurisdiction over engineering colleges located within general universities.
AICTE.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on the MoE for national funding and colleges for compliance documentation.

UGC.name: University Grants Commission
UGC.role: Statutory body maintaining standards of university education.
UGC.authority_scope: Determines higher education standards, distributes development grants, recognizes universities.
UGC.authority_limits: Regulates universities and colleges only. No jurisdiction over primary or secondary schooling.
UGC.policy_authority: High (higher education policies, credit frameworks like ABC).
UGC.operational_authority: Medium (direct regulation via state and central university structures).
UGC.known_ambiguity_regions: Overlapping jurisdiction with professional councils (e.g., AICTE, BCI, MCI) in university departments.
UGC.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on state assemblies and Parliament to establish universities under law.

NIOS.name: National Institute of Open Schooling
NIOS.role: National open school board for distance education.
NIOS.authority_scope: Conducts examinations, designs curricula for distance learners up to pre-degree level.
NIOS.authority_limits: Can only enrol distance learners. Cannot operate standard physical day schools.
NIOS.policy_authority: Medium (translates national curriculum to open distance learning format).
NIOS.operational_authority: High (evaluates distance students, manages study centers).
NIOS.known_ambiguity_regions: Equivalency recognition of NIOS certificates in certain state-level government jobs.
NIOS.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on state boards for physical schools used as examination centers.

IndiaAI.name: National AI Portal and Programme (IndiaAI)
IndiaAI.role: National initiative for coordinating AI policy and technology deployment.
IndiaAI.authority_scope: Drafting AI safety standards, coordinating AI in school pilots, managing computing infrastructure allocations.
IndiaAI.authority_limits: No regulatory enforcement power. Functions as an advisory and execution agency.
IndiaAI.announcement_authority: Medium (releases AI benchmarks, pilot projects announcements).
IndiaAI.policy_authority: Medium-High (authors AI draft ethics policies, curriculum outlines).
IndiaAI.operational_authority: Low (no physical execution authority over educational departments).
IndiaAI.procurement_authority: Medium (grants allocations for AI research labs).
IndiaAI.deployment_authority: Low (must partner with MoE/CBSE for school deployments).
IndiaAI.known_ambiguity_regions: Boundaries of AI curriculum scope vs standard computer science curricula designed by NCERT.
IndiaAI.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on MeitY (Ministry of Electronics & IT) for structural budgets.

DIKSHA.name: Digital Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing (DIKSHA)
DIKSHA.role: National digital learning platform hosting textbooks, videos, and worksheets.
DIKSHA.authority_scope: Standardizes digital content taxonomies, hosts state-level QR code resources.
DIKSHA.authority_limits: Platform-only. No authority to create its own curriculum; hosts only authorized curriculum resources.
DIKSHA.announcement_authority: Medium (platform features updates, national QR metrics).
DIKSHA.policy_authority: Low (platform technical standards only, no academic policy authority).
DIKSHA.operational_authority: Medium (controls platform access, data telemetry pipelines).
DIKSHA.procurement_authority: High (large hosting and platform development contracts).
DIKSHA.known_ambiguity_regions: Ownership of state-contributed teacher data vs central government database access.
DIKSHA.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on state SCERTs to upload state-specific content mapping to local QR codes.

PARAKH.name: Performance Assessment, Review, and Analysis of Knowledge for Holistic Development
PARAKH.role: National assessment center setting standards for student evaluation.
PARAKH.authority_scope: Standardizes student evaluation metrics, removes assessment disparities across national and state boards.
PARAKH.authority_limits: Advisory and standards-setting. Cannot directly run state board examinations.
PARAKH.policy_authority: High (assessment policy design, national benchmark frameworks).
PARAKH.known_ambiguity_regions: Resistance from independent state boards regarding centralized assessment standardization rules.
PARAKH.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on state boards to adopt and execute PARAKH-aligned question formats.

Balbharati.name: Maharashtra State Bureau of Textbook Production and Curriculum Research
Balbharati.role: Authoritative state-level textbook publishing and curriculum research bureau.
Balbharati.authority_scope: Writes and publishes state-aligned textbooks for Standard 1 to 12 across 8 languages.
Balbharati.authority_limits: Limited to Maharashtra State Board schools. Cannot regulate other boards.
Balbharati.policy_authority: High (curriculum design matching Maharashtra State syllabus).
Balbharati.operational_authority: Medium-High (administers textbook distributions, coordinates state curriculum shifts).
Balbharati.procurement_authority: High (large-scale printing and digital licensing agreements).
Balbharati.known_ambiguity_regions: Overlapping boundaries with MSCERT regarding syllabus revision timelines and standards.
Balbharati.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on the Maharashtra State Education Department for regulatory execution decrees.

MSCERT.name: Maharashtra State Council of Educational Research and Training
MSCERT.role: State-level academic advisory and teacher training authority in Maharashtra.
MSCERT.authority_scope: Formulates state curriculum frameworks (SCF), plans teacher training, monitors educational quality.
MSCERT.authority_limits: Advisory authority. Must coordinate with the School Education Department for administrative mandates.
MSCERT.announcement_authority: Medium-High (State SCF releases, teacher training mandates).
MSCERT.policy_authority: High (Maharashtra state education curriculum policy authorship).
MSCERT.operational_authority: Medium (operates state teacher training colleges and DIETs).
MSCERT.known_ambiguity_regions: Tensions with Balbharati regarding who owns the final sign-off on curriculum edits.
MSCERT.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on NCERT for alignment with national standards and MoE for funding.

MSBSHSE.name: Maharashtra State Board of Secondary and Higher Secondary Education
MSBSHSE.role: Secondary and Higher Secondary education board of Maharashtra.
MSBSHSE.authority_scope: Conducts SSC (Class 10) and HSC (Class 12) board examinations for millions of students.
MSBSHSE.authority_limits: Strictly limited to schools affiliated with the Maharashtra State Board. Cannot govern CBSE/ICSE schools.
MSBSHSE.policy_authority: Medium (administers board curricula, establishes state affiliation rules).
MSBSHSE.operational_authority: High (runs exam centers, certifies students, manages affiliation records).
MSBSHSE.deployment_authority: High (reaches every affiliated school in Maharashtra).
MSBSHSE.known_ambiguity_regions: Jurisdiction over private state board junior colleges that operate integrated coaching tracks.
MSBSHSE.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on Balbharati for curriculum textbook prints and School Education Department for final guidelines.

MPSP.name: Maharashtra Prathmik Shikshan Parishad
MPSP.role: State implementation agency for Samagra Shiksha.
MPSP.authority_scope: Manages state primary and secondary school development budgets, procures educational technology at scale.
MPSP.authority_limits: Focuses strictly on public schools and budget implementations. Cannot rewrite state curricula.
MPSP.announcement_authority: Medium (school budget announcements, digital tender releases).
MPSP.operational_authority: High (direct control over educational funding and infrastructure delivery).
MPSP.procurement_authority: High (procures smart classrooms, tablets, and LMS systems for Maharashtra).
MPSP.deployment_authority: High (executes deployment across all public primary/secondary schools).
MPSP.known_ambiguity_regions: Overlapping project coordinates with MahaIT regarding technology procurement execution.
MPSP.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on central Samagra Shiksha funds release and district collectors for local school deployment audit.

DEIT&AI.name: Department of Electronics, Information Technology & Artificial Intelligence (Maharashtra)
DEIT&AI.role: State department coordinating digital governance and technology adoption.
DEIT&AI.authority_scope: Setting state IT infrastructure standards, supervising data privacy guidelines, approving smart city and school AI integrations.
DEIT&AI.authority_limits: Regulatory authority over IT infrastructure only. No authority over curriculum or teacher administration.
DEIT&AI.policy_authority: Medium-High (authors state AI integration rules, cloud hosting policies).
DEIT&AI.operational_authority: Medium (supervises state government datacenters and network infrastructure).
DEIT&AI.procurement_authority: High (central IT software and cloud procurement).
DEIT&AI.deployment_authority: Medium (IT networks across state offices and public kiosks).
DEIT&AI.known_ambiguity_regions: Demarcation of educational data hosting rules vs national DPDP standards.
DEIT&AI.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on state budget allocations and MahaIT for execution.

MahaIT.name: Maharashtra Information Technology Corporation
MahaIT.role: Nodal agency for IT infrastructure implementation in Maharashtra.
MahaIT.authority_scope: Executes digital projects, operates the state portal (MahaOnline), manages state cloud infrastructure.
MahaIT.authority_limits: Execution agency. Cannot create policies or govern educational standards; acts as technical vendor for state departments.
MahaIT.operational_authority: High (operates government hosting, cloud storage, and state data networks).
MahaIT.procurement_authority: High (nears monopoly on state government IT and software contracts).
MahaIT.deployment_authority: High (technical backend for all state-wide digital platforms).
MahaIT.known_ambiguity_regions: Tensions with individual department IT wings regarding procurement authority and vendor choices.
MahaIT.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on parent departments to articulate software functional specifications.

MKCL.name: Maharashtra Knowledge Corporation Limited
MKCL.role: Public limited company promoted by Department of Higher and Technical Education.
MKCL.authority_scope: Implements IT literacy programmes (MS-CIT), designs online admission systems, conducts digital learning programs.
MKCL.authority_limits: Functions as a public-private vendor. No regulatory or policy enforcement authority.
MKCL.announcement_authority: Medium (IT literacy campaigns, admission system schedules).
MKCL.policy_authority: Low (follows state higher and technical education mandates).
MKCL.operational_authority: High (administers training networks, conducts admissions, operates learning management platforms).
MKCL.deployment_authority: High (extensive networks of local computer learning centers across Maharashtra).
MKCL.known_ambiguity_regions: Jurisdiction of MKCL admission processes vs independent university registration portals.
MKCL.known_dependency_surfaces: Dependent on the Department of Higher & Technical Education for exclusive contract allocations.
